title: Establish liminal crate with foundation types and error taxonomy
purpose: Bootstrap the liminal crate with Cargo.toml, the public API surface in lib.rs, the foundational channel types (ChannelMode, ChannelConfig, ChannelHandle), and the error taxonomy. This is the leaf dependency for every other brief in the cluster.
task: Create the liminal crate from scratch. Define the core types that everything else depends on: ChannelMode (Ephemeral/Durable), ChannelConfig (name, schema, mode — no silent defaults per P6), ChannelHandle (the caller-facing handle for publish/subscribe/close). Define LiminalError covering channel, conversation, schema, and delivery failure cases, implementing std::error::Error and Debug. Set up lib.rs to re-export the channel, conversation, envelope, and error modules. This brief creates the skeleton — channel actor behaviour (LIM-002), schema validation (LIM-003), and conversation actor (LIM-004) build on it.

requirements.title: Create crate manifest with beamr dependency
requirements.spec: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml SHALL declare the liminal crate with beamr as a dependency. The crate SHALL use the workspace edition, rust-version, and lint settings. The crate SHALL NOT depend on haematite, aion, or any network library — those are separate clusters.
acceptance: Cargo.toml exists at crates/liminal/Cargo.toml with [package] name = "liminal"
beamr appears in [dependencies] with features = ["json"]
Workspace lints inherited via [lints] workspace = true
No dependency on haematite, aion, tokio, axum, or tonic

files: Cargo.toml:1-22 — workspace members already include crates/liminal; shared edition/package metadata and strict lint settings live here, but no workspace rust-version is currently defined.

crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:1-14 — existing liminal manifest already has package name, workspace edition/license/repository/authors, beamr dependency, and workspace lints; beamr is currently plain "0.6.1" without features.
crates/liminal-server/Cargo.toml:1-14 — neighboring crate shows the same manifest convention: workspace package fields plus [lints] workspace = true.
Cargo.lock:571-575 — current liminal lock entry depends only on beamr; update via cargo, not by hand.
Cargo.lock:30-54 — beamr 0.6.1 transitively pulls tokio; this is not a direct liminal dependency and should not be treated as the R1 forbidden-dependency violation.
context: Workspace membership is already present for crates/liminal, so the likely R1 change is correcting the liminal crate manifest rather than adding the member.
The crate follows workspace inheritance for edition/license/repository/authors and [lints] workspace = true; preserve that style rather than hardcoding package metadata locally.
The brief says to use workspace rust-version, but root Cargo.toml currently has no [workspace.package].rust-version; do not invent a crate-local value unless the workspace is updated consistently.
R5’s #[derive(thiserror::Error)] requires a direct thiserror dependency in crates/liminal/Cargo.toml; thiserror is not in the forbidden list.
scout.approach: Update crates/liminal/Cargo.toml in place: keep the existing package metadata inheritance and [lints] workspace = true, convert beamr to table syntax with version 0.6.1 and features = ["json"], and add thiserror for the error enum. Do not add direct haematite, aion, tokio, axum, tonic, or any network/persistence dependency. Only touch root Cargo.toml if the implementation chooses to introduce a workspace rust-version; if so, add it at [workspace.package] and inherit it rather than hardcoding a local value.
scout.notes: Cargo.lock should be regenerated by cargo check/build, not edited by hand. If acceptance is mechanical about rust-version.workspace = true, root Cargo.toml must first gain a workspace rust-version; edition 2024 implies a recent compiler, but the repo currently does not declare the exact MSRV.

files_changed.note: Added workspace rust-version so liminal can inherit edition and rust-version from workspace metadata as specified.
Inherited workspace rust-version/lints, enabled beamr json feature, and added thiserror for the required error derive.
Regenerated by cargo to reflect beamr json feature transitive additions and thiserror.

dev.how: The liminal crate manifest now declares package name liminal, inherits workspace edition/rust-version/lints, uses beamr = { version = "0.6.1", features = ["json"] }, and adds only thiserror as the direct supporting dependency for R5. The manifest has no direct haematite, aion, tokio, axum, tonic, persistence, or network dependencies.

checklist.note: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml exists and declares beamr with the required json feature.

acceptance.criterion: Cargo.toml exists at crates/liminal/Cargo.toml with [package] name = "liminal"
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:1-3 contains [package] and name = "liminal".

acceptance.criterion: beamr appears in [dependencies] with features = ["json"]
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-12 declares beamr under [dependencies] with features = ["json"]. Hardened line also sets default-features = false and optional = true to avoid compiling beamr's default net/tokio stack in LIM-001.

acceptance.criterion: Workspace lints inherited via [lints] workspace = true
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:15-16 has [lints] workspace = true; workspace lint definitions are in Cargo.toml:14-23.

acceptance.criterion: No dependency on haematite, aion, tokio, axum, or tonic
acceptance.evidence: After harden, cargo tree -p liminal --depth 2 shows only thiserror in the default graph. Cargo.lock search found only beamr and liminal among checked names; no haematite/aion/tokio/axum/tonic entries. Cargo.lock:24-38 beamr's json/default-disabled entry excludes tokio.

issues: The original actual diff used beamr = { version = "0.6.1", features = ["json"] }, which enabled beamr default features. Beamr defaults include net/threads and pulled tokio into Cargo.lock, violating the no-network/no-tokio boundary.
fixes: Changed crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:12 to beamr = { version = "0.6.1", default-features = false, features = ["json"], optional = true }.
Regenerated Cargo.lock; the beamr lock entry now includes json dependencies only and removes tokio/network/JIT/default-feature transitive packages from liminal's default dependency graph.

requirements.title: Define ChannelMode enum
requirements.spec: ChannelMode SHALL have exactly two variants: Ephemeral (in-memory only, no persistence) and Durable (backed by haematite, survives restart). The enum SHALL derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.
acceptance: ChannelMode::Ephemeral and ChannelMode::Durable are the only variants
ChannelMode derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
ChannelMode is re-exported from lib.rs

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:1-1 — currently empty target file for ChannelMode and related channel foundation types.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-5 — crate root declares modules but does not yet re-export ChannelMode.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-1 — currently only a comment; should become module declarations/re-exports for channel types.
docs/design/core/design.json:21-31 — P4 and P6 establish explicit durability choice and no silent defaults.
docs/design/core/design.json:97-99 — design structure assigns ChannelConfig, ChannelMode, and ChannelHandle to channel/types.rs for LIM-001.
context: ChannelMode acceptance is exact: only Ephemeral and Durable, deriving Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.
Durable is a public mode marker only in this brief; do not add haematite integration or even a haematite dependency to represent it.
All public types must implement Debug, and ChannelMode must be re-exported from lib.rs, ideally through channel/mod.rs as well for module ergonomics.
Avoid adding Default or extra variants; exact variant count is part of the acceptance criteria.
scout.approach: Define ChannelMode in crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs as a small public enum with exactly Ephemeral and Durable and the required derives. Re-export it from crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs with a pub use, then from crates/liminal/src/lib.rs with pub use channel::ChannelMode or pub use channel::types::ChannelMode. Keep the enum declarative only; no behavior, persistence hooks, or actor references are needed for LIM-001.
scout.notes: Do not add serde derives, numeric reprs, feature gates, or variant data unless a later brief asks for them; extra surface can make exact-variant tests fail or imply out-of-scope durability behavior.

files_changed.note: Added the ChannelMode enum.
Re-exported ChannelMode through the channel module.
Re-exported ChannelMode from the crate root.

dev.how: ChannelMode is a public enum with exactly Ephemeral and Durable variants and derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq. No behavior, serde derives, default implementation, or durability integration was added.

checklist.note: ChannelMode contains only Ephemeral and Durable.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelMode::Ephemeral and ChannelMode::Durable are the only variants
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:6-10 defines pub enum ChannelMode with only Ephemeral and Durable variants.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelMode derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:5 has #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)].

acceptance.criterion: ChannelMode is re-exported from lib.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:7 re-exports ChannelMode from channel; crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:5 re-exports it from types.

requirements.title: Define ChannelConfig requiring explicit name, schema, and mode
requirements.spec: ChannelConfig SHALL require a channel name (String), a schema definition (SchemaRef — opaque reference, defined fully in LIM-003), and a ChannelMode. ChannelConfig SHALL NOT implement Default — every field is required, no silent defaults (P6). A builder pattern MAY be used if it enforces all-fields-required.
acceptance: ChannelConfig has fields: name (String), schema (SchemaRef), mode (ChannelMode)
ChannelConfig does NOT implement Default
ChannelConfig::new(name, schema, mode) constructor requires all three arguments
SchemaRef is a placeholder type (unit struct or newtype) — LIM-003 fills in the real definition

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:1-1 — target for SchemaRef placeholder, ChannelConfig, and ChannelConfig::new.
crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:1-1 — empty future schema module; LIM-003 owns real schema definition and validation, so do not fill it for R3.
docs/design/core/design.json:21-31 — P4/P6 require explicit mode and no silent defaults for channel creation.
docs/design/core/design.json:102-104 — schema definition/validation/evolution is explicitly assigned to LIM-003, not this brief.
docs/design/core/stories.json:5-9 — S1 frames ChannelConfig as part of a simple typed-channel creation experience.
context: ChannelConfig must have name: String, schema: SchemaRef, and mode: ChannelMode, with no Default implementation and no optional/silent defaults.
SchemaRef should be an opaque placeholder in this brief; a unit struct or lightweight newtype is enough so LIM-003 can replace/fill it later.
Because public types must implement Debug, derive Debug on both ChannelConfig and SchemaRef; if deriving Eq/PartialEq/Clone for ChannelConfig, SchemaRef must derive matching traits.
Strict clippy pedantic lints may warn on constructors returning Self; annotate ChannelConfig::new with #[must_use].
scout.approach: In channel/types.rs, add a public SchemaRef placeholder with minimal derives, then define public ChannelConfig with the three required fields and a constructor `pub fn new(name: String, schema: SchemaRef, mode: ChannelMode) -> Self`. Do not implement Default and do not use a builder unless it is typestate-enforced; the direct constructor is simpler and matches acceptance. Re-export ChannelConfig from channel/mod.rs and lib.rs alongside ChannelMode.
scout.notes: Using `impl Into<String>` for the constructor would be ergonomic, but an exact `String` parameter is less likely to surprise mechanical signature checks. Keep SchemaRef in types.rs for now unless the implementer also wires channel/schema.rs carefully without over-scoping LIM-003.

files_changed.note: Added SchemaRef placeholder, ChannelConfig fields, and all-fields-required constructor.
Re-exported ChannelConfig and SchemaRef.
Re-exported ChannelConfig from the crate root.

dev.how: ChannelConfig now has public name: String, schema: SchemaRef, and mode: ChannelMode fields plus ChannelConfig::new(name: String, schema: SchemaRef, mode: ChannelMode) -> Self. SchemaRef is an opaque unit struct placeholder for LIM-003. ChannelConfig deliberately does not implement Default, preserving explicit all-fields-required configuration.

checklist.note: ChannelConfig requires name, schema, and mode via fields and constructor; no Default implementation was added.

stories.note: Developers can construct typed channel configuration with a single ChannelConfig::new call, with schema and mode explicit.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelConfig has fields: name (String), schema (SchemaRef), mode (ChannelMode)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:19-26 defines ChannelConfig with pub name: String, pub schema: SchemaRef, and pub mode: ChannelMode.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelConfig does NOT implement Default
acceptance.evidence: Search for Default/impl Default/derive Default in crates/liminal/src returned no matches; ChannelConfig derives only Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq at crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:18.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelConfig::new(name, schema, mode) constructor requires all three arguments
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:28-33 defines pub const fn new(name: String, schema: SchemaRef, mode: ChannelMode) -> Self and initializes all three fields.

acceptance.criterion: SchemaRef is a placeholder type (unit struct or newtype) — LIM-003 fills in the real definition
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:13-15 defines SchemaRef as an opaque unit struct placeholder.

requirements.title: Define ChannelHandle with publish, subscribe, and close
requirements.spec: ChannelHandle SHALL expose publish, subscribe, and close methods as the public API for interacting with a channel. publish SHALL accept a message payload and return a Result. subscribe SHALL return a SubscriptionHandle. close SHALL signal the channel actor to shut down gracefully. ChannelHandle SHALL be Clone and Send + Sync (it is a handle to a process, not the process itself).
acceptance: ChannelHandle has publish(&self, payload) -> Result<(), LiminalError> method signature
ChannelHandle has subscribe(&self) -> Result<SubscriptionHandle, LiminalError> method signature
ChannelHandle has close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError> method signature
ChannelHandle is Clone + Send + Sync
SubscriptionHandle is defined as a stub type in channel/subscription.rs

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:1-1 — target for ChannelHandle and its publish/subscribe/close methods.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:1-1 — currently empty target for the SubscriptionHandle stub type.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-1 — LiminalError lives here and is the Result error type for ChannelHandle methods.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-1 — should expose types and subscription modules/re-exports so ChannelHandle can refer to SubscriptionHandle cleanly.
docs/design/core/design.json:75-110 — structure maps channel/types.rs and channel/subscription.rs to LIM-001/LIM-002 boundaries.
context: ChannelHandle must be Clone + Send + Sync and Debug; a private Arc-backed marker or zero-sized handle can satisfy that without implementing actor behavior.
publish should accept a payload and return Result<(), LiminalError>; a generic payload parameter avoids committing to schema/envelope types before LIM-003.
subscribe returns a SubscriptionHandle stub from channel/subscription.rs; no subscriber management, filters, mailbox delivery, or actor linking belongs in LIM-001.
The lint set denies panic/unwrap/expect, so stubs must not use todo!(), unimplemented!(), panic!(), unwrap(), or expect().
scout.approach: Define a minimal public SubscriptionHandle stub in channel/subscription.rs with Debug and Clone, plus a crate-visible constructor if needed. Define ChannelHandle in channel/types.rs with a private field whose type is naturally Send + Sync, derive Clone and Debug, and implement the three public methods with the required Result signatures. Since actor behavior is out of scope, make the bodies deterministic compile-time stubs, use `_payload`/`let _ = self;` to avoid unused warnings, and return Ok values or a clearly documented LiminalError rather than panicking.
scout.notes: If the handle uses `Arc<()>`, it communicates cloneable process-handle semantics without requiring beamr APIs yet. Avoid importing haematite, creating process supervisors, performing schema validation, or serializing payloads; those are later briefs.

files_changed.note: Added ChannelHandle with publish, subscribe, and close methods returning Result values.
Defined a stub SubscriptionHandle.
Declared subscription module and re-exported ChannelHandle and SubscriptionHandle.
Re-exported ChannelHandle from the crate root.

dev.how: ChannelHandle is a Clone + Debug zero-sized process-handle stub, which is Send + Sync by construction. It exposes publish<Payload>(&self, payload: Payload) -> Result<(), LiminalError>, subscribe(&self) -> Result<SubscriptionHandle, LiminalError>, and close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError>. The method bodies are deterministic no-op stubs returning Ok values and do not implement actor behavior, schema validation, subscriber management, persistence, or shutdown signaling.
dev.deviation: The scout approach suggested a private Arc-backed marker field as one option. I initially used that, but clippy rejected no-op field touch patterns and warned on the subscription field; I switched ChannelHandle and SubscriptionHandle to zero-sized stubs, which the scout context also allowed and which still satisfies Clone + Send + Sync without adding behavior.

checklist.note: ChannelHandle provides publish, subscribe, and close operations with LiminalError Result returns.

stories.note: The public API surface now includes a channel handle that can publish, subscribe, and close once later actor briefs wire real behavior.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle has publish(&self, payload) -> Result<(), LiminalError> method signature
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:47-51 defines pub fn publish<Payload>(&self, payload: Payload) -> Result<(), LiminalError>.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle has subscribe(&self) -> Result<SubscriptionHandle, LiminalError> method signature
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:59-61 defines pub const fn subscribe(&self) -> Result<SubscriptionHandle, LiminalError>.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle has close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError> method signature
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:69-71 defines pub const fn close(&self) -> Result<(), LiminalError>.

acceptance.criterion: ChannelHandle is Clone + Send + Sync
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:37-38 derives Clone and defines ChannelHandle as a fieldless unit struct; fieldless public structs auto-implement Send + Sync. cargo check -p liminal passes.

acceptance.criterion: SubscriptionHandle is defined as a stub type in channel/subscription.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:1-3 defines a stub pub struct SubscriptionHandle with Clone + Debug.

requirements.title: Define LiminalError enum with thiserror
requirements.spec: LiminalError SHALL cover: ChannelNotFound, ChannelClosed, SchemaMismatch, PublishFailed, SubscriptionFailed, ConversationFailed, ConversationTimeout, ParticipantCrashed, DeliveryFailed. Each variant SHALL carry a descriptive message string. LiminalError SHALL implement std::error::Error via thiserror and Debug.
acceptance: LiminalError is defined in crates/liminal/src/error.rs
All nine variants listed are present with message fields
LiminalError derives Debug and uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)]
LiminalError is re-exported from lib.rs

files: crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-1 — currently empty target for LiminalError.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:10-14 — dependency section currently lacks thiserror, which is required for #[derive(thiserror::Error)].
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-5 — crate root currently declares error module but does not re-export LiminalError.
docs/design/core/checklist.json:73-76 — C29/C30 require broad error coverage and std::error::Error + Debug.
docs/design/core/design.json:137-139 — design structure assigns the LiminalError taxonomy to crates/liminal/src/error.rs for LIM-001.
context: LiminalError must include exactly the nine named failure categories from the brief and each variant must carry a descriptive message string.
Use `#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]` so the enum implements std::error::Error through thiserror and satisfies CN4.
Named-field variants like `{ message: String }` are the safest reading of “message fields” and make construction explicit for tests/future code.
The error type is part of the public API and must be re-exported from lib.rs.
scout.approach: Add thiserror to crates/liminal/Cargo.toml, then define `pub enum LiminalError` in error.rs with the nine variants: ChannelNotFound, ChannelClosed, SchemaMismatch, PublishFailed, SubscriptionFailed, ConversationFailed, ConversationTimeout, ParticipantCrashed, and DeliveryFailed. Give each variant a `message: String` field and an `#[error("{message}")]` display annotation. Re-export LiminalError from lib.rs after declaring the error module.
scout.notes: Do not collapse variants into generic channel/conversation errors; later briefs need the taxonomy. Do not add sources or backtraces unless requested, because acceptance is specifically about message strings and thiserror-derived Error/Debug.

files_changed.note: Defined the LiminalError thiserror enum with all required variants and message fields.
Added thiserror dependency.
Re-exported LiminalError from the crate root.
Regenerated by cargo to include thiserror and thiserror-impl.

dev.how: LiminalError is defined in crates/liminal/src/error.rs with #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]. It includes ChannelNotFound, ChannelClosed, SchemaMismatch, PublishFailed, SubscriptionFailed, ConversationFailed, ConversationTimeout, ParticipantCrashed, and DeliveryFailed, each carrying a named message: String field and displaying that message.

checklist.note: The enum covers channel, conversation, schema, participant crash, publish/subscription, and delivery failures.
The enum derives Debug and thiserror::Error, implementing std::error::Error.

acceptance.criterion: LiminalError is defined in crates/liminal/src/error.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-3 defines pub enum LiminalError.

acceptance.criterion: All nine variants listed are present with message fields
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/error.rs:4-38 contains ChannelNotFound, ChannelClosed, SchemaMismatch, PublishFailed, SubscriptionFailed, ConversationFailed, ConversationTimeout, ParticipantCrashed, and DeliveryFailed; each variant has { message: String }.

acceptance.criterion: LiminalError derives Debug and uses #[derive(thiserror::Error)]
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/error.rs:2 has #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)].

acceptance.criterion: LiminalError is re-exported from lib.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:8 re-exports LiminalError.

requirements.title: Set up lib.rs with module declarations and re-exports
requirements.spec: lib.rs SHALL declare pub mod for channel, conversation, envelope, error, and causal modules. lib.rs SHALL re-export the primary public types: ChannelMode, ChannelConfig, ChannelHandle, LiminalError. lib.rs SHALL contain ONLY module declarations and re-exports — no logic (CN3).
acceptance: lib.rs declares pub mod channel, conversation, envelope, error, causal
lib.rs re-exports ChannelMode, ChannelConfig, ChannelHandle, LiminalError
lib.rs contains zero function definitions, zero trait definitions, zero struct definitions
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs exists with pub mod types, subscription
crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs exists as a placeholder (populated by LIM-004)
cargo check -p liminal passes

files: crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-5 — currently declares pub mod channel/conversation/envelope/causal/error but lacks the required primary type re-exports.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-1 — currently a comment; acceptance requires pub mod types and pub mod subscription, with re-exports strongly recommended.
crates/liminal/src/conversation/mod.rs:1-1 — currently a comment placeholder for LIM-004; keep it placeholder-only or declarations/re-exports only.
crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:1-1 — empty placeholder module declared by lib.rs; leave behavior to later briefs.
crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:1-1 — empty placeholder module declared by lib.rs; leave causal implementation to LIM-006.
context: lib.rs already has the required module declarations, but R6 also requires re-exporting ChannelMode, ChannelConfig, ChannelHandle, and LiminalError.
CN3 applies to mod.rs files, and R6 explicitly applies the same no-logic rule to lib.rs: no functions, traits, structs, or behavior in the crate root.
channel/mod.rs should contain only `pub mod types;`, `pub mod subscription;`, and `pub use ...` lines; do not put type definitions in mod.rs.
There are extra placeholder directories under liminal/src such as aion, durability, metrics, pressure, routing, and tracing; do not expose them from lib.rs for this core skeleton brief.
scout.approach: Make lib.rs a pure public API surface: declare exactly the required modules and add pub use re-exports for ChannelConfig, ChannelHandle, ChannelMode, and LiminalError. Make channel/mod.rs a pure module root declaring types and subscription and re-exporting ChannelConfig/ChannelHandle/ChannelMode/SchemaRef/SubscriptionHandle as appropriate. Leave conversation/mod.rs, envelope.rs, and causal.rs as empty placeholders or declaration-only files so cargo check succeeds without implementing future briefs.
scout.notes: Comments in mod.rs are harmless to the compiler but the design says mod.rs contains only declarations/re-exports, so the safest implementation is to remove comment-only placeholders or replace them with actual pub mod/pub use lines. Keep lib.rs from becoming a dumping ground; all structs/enums belong in their leaf modules.

files_changed.note: Declared required public modules and re-exported primary public types only.
Declared channel types and subscription modules with re-exports only.
Kept as an empty placeholder for LIM-004 with no logic.
Existing empty placeholder remains declared by lib.rs.
Existing empty placeholder remains declared by lib.rs.

Future placeholder modules remain empty so no future-brief logic was introduced.

checklist.note: The crate root declares and re-exports the required modules and primary public types; cargo check -p liminal passes.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement channel actor behaviour — that is LIM-002.
SHALL NOT implement schema validation logic — that is LIM-003.
SHALL NOT implement conversation actor — that is LIM-004.
SHALL NOT add any persistence or haematite integration — those are durability cluster briefs.
SHALL NOT add any network or wire protocol code — that is the protocol cluster.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
lib.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
